title: Smoky Crispy Korean Mackerel Gui
description: A smoky, crispy, high-protein Korean busan dish featuring a creamy glaze and deep-fried mackerel.
instructions: Pat mackerel fillets dry and season with salt and black pepper.
In a small bowl whisk soy sauce, gochujang, honey, rice vinegar, smoked paprika, heavy cream, and butter until smooth.
Heat a skillet over medium-high and melt butter.
Place fillets skin side down and sear for 4 minutes until skin crisp.
Flip fillets and cook for 2 minutes, then pour glaze over fish and let it thicken.
Meanwhile, pour the remaining glaze into a shallow pan and heat until bubbling, then add gochugaru and sesame seeds and stir.
Drizzle the hot glaze over the seared fillets and cook for another minute.
Serve immediately with steamed rice and a squeeze of lemon if desired.
Garnish with extra toasted sesame seeds and chopped green onions.
Store leftovers in an airtight container for up to 2 days.
